pages.raw_text:
Belden, teacher; Mrs. Jennie Jones,
chairman.


ST. Patrick's dance, at Masonic
temple; every one invited, as the
proceeds are to go to the new club
house fund.


Friday, March 18-Junior Ger
man, third floor Palace, 1:45 to
2:45 p. M. Mrs. Prayer, teacher.
Senior French, 2:45 to 3:45 D. m,
Palace. Professor LIE Gras, teacher.
Senior German, to p. M, Palace.
Mrs. Prayer, teacher.


Saturday, March 19-Milinery,
$3.50 a. IN. to IL m, at the Palace.
China painting, to p. M, studio
of teacher, Mrs. M. Shide, 1920
First avenue. Mrs. John r. Cassin,
chairman.


Mrs. ID. B. Fotheringham has very
kindly thrown open her home to
the Woman's club for series of
card parties, the proceeds from
which will go to the new club house
fund. Handsome prizes will be
given each day and dainty lunch
con served by Mrs. Fotheringham.
On Wednesday, March 16, bridge
whist will be played. On Thursday,
March II. five hundred. On Fri
day. March IS, straight whist. All
those who plan to come will please
communicate with Mrs. ID. b.
Fotheringham in order that suffi-
cient tables may be provided and a
place reserved.
